extra.reasoning: Property: NOT (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a is set true ONLY by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which fires exactly when all 12 conditions hold (ckd_dialysis, seizure_history, immunosuppressed, diabetes, g6pd_deficiency, pregnancy, polypharmacy, elderly, anticoagulated, hypertension, peptic_ulcer, heart_failure); otherwise rec_drug_a defaults to false. rec_drug_b can be set true by rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50, IF ckd_dialysis) but is set false by guard_suppress_b (priority 90, IF the identical 12-condition conjunction). The guard's condition is exactly the same conjunction that makes rec_drug_a true, and its priority 90 beats the trigger's priority 50. Therefore, in every input where rec_drug_a becomes true (full conjunction holds), guard_suppress_b also fires and forces rec_drug_b := false, overriding rec_b_from_trigger. Case analysis: (1) conjunction holds -> a=true, b=false (guard wins); (2) conjunction fails but ckd_dialysis true -> a=false (default), b=true; (3) otherwise -> a=false. In no case are both true. renal_impairment and the other unused inputs only affect rec_drug_c, which is irrelevant to this property. The property holds over the entire input space.
